The bioactive constituents of some orchid species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with orchids include teas, tinctures, and capsules, although the use and preparation methods may vary depending on the specific species and cultural tradition.
